subject: WAITA-EACRI yam proposal — draft sections
snippet: Hi Layla — sharing my draft of the methodology section...
body:
Hi Layla,

Sharing my draft of the methodology section for the WAITA-EACRI yam improvement proposal. I focused on the multi-location trial design across Nairobi and Enugu sites.

Can you review the budget justification before our Wednesday call? The Oct 2 submission deadline is creeping up.

Warm regards,
Amina

subject: Re: WAITA-EACRI yam proposal — draft sections
snippet: Thanks Amina, methodology looks solid. Two points on the budget...
body:
Thanks Amina, the methodology looks solid. Two points on the budget:
1. We under-costed field assistant stipends for the Udi sites — let's add 15%.
2. WAITA caps equipment at 20% of total; we're slightly over.

I'll have the revised justification to you before Wednesday 3pm WAT.

Best,
Layla

subject: Re: WAITA-EACRI yam proposal — draft sections
snippet: Agreed on both. See you Wednesday...
body: Agreed on both. I'll trim the equipment line and reallocate to consumables. See you Wednesday on the call.\n\nAmina

subject: WAADA Year 2 progress report — reminder
snippet: Dear Dr. McBride, the WAADA Year 2 narrative report is due...
body:
Dear Dr. McBride,

This is a reminder that the WAADA cassava biofortification Year 2 narrative and financial report is due to the grants office by 30 June. Please submit through the research portal.

Let me know if you need the updated expenditure template.

Regards,
Rebecca Okoro
Grants & Research Admin, NNU

subject: Re: WAADA Year 2 progress report — reminder
snippet: Thank you Rebecca, please send the updated template...
body:
Thank you Rebecca,

Please do send the updated expenditure template. The Udi trial data is being finalised this week, so the narrative will be ready well ahead of the deadline.

Kind regards,
Layla McBride

subject: Cassava beta-carotene assays — batch 3 results
snippet: Layla — batch 3 HPLC results are in, looking strong...
body:
Layla,

Batch 3 HPLC results are in and the beta-carotene levels in the new clones are looking strong — clearly above the check varieties. I've dropped the raw CSVs in the shared Drive folder.

Should we hold these for the paper or share a teaser with Amina's group?

Derek

subject: Re: Cassava beta-carotene assays — batch 3 results
snippet: Great news. Let's keep batch 3 internal for now...
body:
Great news, Derek. Let's keep batch 3 internal until we lock the manuscript — I don't want preliminary numbers circulating before peer review.

I'll run the stats in R this weekend and we can discuss Tuesday.

Layla

subject: Q3 farmer training workshop — Nsukka LGA dates
snippet: Good morning Dr. McBride, proposing dates for the next workshop...
body:
Good morning Dr. McBride,

Proposing dates for the Q3 farmer training workshop in Nsukka LGA. We're looking at the second week of July to fit the cooperative calendar. Expecting around 90 farmers this round.

Chidi will handle logistics and seed-kit distribution. Could you confirm your availability?

Regards,
Ngozi Eze
ESFES Programme Coordinator

subject: Re: Q3 farmer training workshop — Nsukka LGA dates
snippet: July 9th and 10th work for me...
body:
Good morning Ngozi,

July 9th and 10th work well for me. I'll prepare the session on cassava agronomy and the demonstration plot walkthrough. Please ask Chidi to confirm the booklet and calendar stock with Mama Nkechi's cooperative.

Thank you,
Layla

subject: West African Journal of Crop Research — submission window
snippet: Dear Dr. McBride, regarding your forthcoming submission...
body:
Dear Dr. McBride,

Thank you for your interest in submitting to the West African Journal of Crop Research. Our autumn issue submission window closes 16 October 2026. Please ensure your manuscript follows the updated author guidelines (attached link).

The article processing charge is USD 150 for accepted papers.

Best regards,
Editorial Office, WAJCR

subject: December visit — flights and the kids
snippet: Hi sweetheart, your dad and I are looking at the calendar...
body:
Hi sweetheart,

Your dad and I are looking at the calendar for your December visit. Are you still thinking arriving around the 20th? Sophia and Elijah's room is ready and Patricia wants to plan a few outings.

Let me know once Marcus confirms his dates so we can book.

Love,
Mom

subject: Re: December visit — flights and the kids
snippet: Yes around the 20th. Marcus is confirming his project handover...
body:
Hi Mom,

Yes, still aiming for around the 20th. Marcus is confirming a project handover at the firm first, but it should be fine. The kids are so excited — Sophia keeps asking about Grandma's garden.

I'll book once flights settle. Give my love to Dad and Patricia.

Layla

subject: Thesis chapter 4 — draft for your review
snippet: Good day Dr. McBride, please find my chapter 4 draft...
body:
Good day Dr. McBride,

Please find attached my Chapter 4 draft covering the genotype-by-environment analysis. I have incorporated the mixed-model approach you suggested.

I would appreciate your feedback before the departmental seminar in early October.

Thank you,
Samuel Attah

subject: Re: Thesis chapter 4 — draft for your review
snippet: Received Samuel. I'll return comments by Friday...
body:
Received, Samuel. Good progress on the mixed model. I'll return tracked comments by Friday — let's meet Tuesday research day to walk through the variance components.

Keep it up.
Dr. McBride

subject: End of term newsletter & fee reminder
snippet: Dear Parent, term ends 18 July. Next term fees of N85,000...
body:
Dear Parent,

The term ends on 18 July. Sophia has had a wonderful term in the nursery class. Next term fees of N85,000 per pupil are due before resumption.

Please see the attached newsletter for the prize-giving day schedule.

Warm regards,
Bright Future Nursery & Primary School

subject: Win a free greenhouse kit — limited offer!!!
snippet: Congratulations! You have been selected...
body:
Congratulations!

You have been pre-selected to win a FREE greenhouse starter kit. Just confirm your details within 24 hours to claim your prize. Click the link below now!

Do not miss out!!!

subject: Abuja visit and Mom's birthday plans
snippet: Hey sis, are we doing a joint gift for Mom this year?...
body:
Hey sis,

Are we doing a joint gift for Mom this year for December? I was thinking we split something nice. Also I might come to Enugu the last weekend of July if my pharmacy schedule allows — would love to see the kids.

Call you Sunday?
Nadia

subject: Re: Abuja visit and Mom's birthday plans
snippet: Yes let's split a gift. Come the last weekend of July...
body:
Yes, let's split a gift — I'll send some ideas. And please come the last weekend of July, the kids would be thrilled and Blessing can help if I'm grading.

We'll talk Sunday at 8pm as usual.
Layla

subject: Crop Science departmental conference — presenter slot
snippet: Dear Layla, you are confirmed to present in the early October session...
body:
Dear Layla,

You are confirmed to present in the early October departmental conference session. Please send your title and abstract to Rebecca by 15 September.

The Dean has asked that biofortification work feature prominently this year given the WAADA funding.

Regards,
Prof. Emeka Okonkwo
Head of Department, Crop Science

subject: Re: Crop Science departmental conference — presenter slot
snippet: Thank you Prof. Okonkwo, I'll present the cassava biofortification field results...
body:
Thank you, Prof. Okonkwo,

I'll present the cassava biofortification field results from the Udi trials. I'll send the title and abstract to Rebecca well before 15 September.

Grateful for the opportunity.
Layla McBride

subject: Miss you! + book recommendation
snippet: Layla!! It's been too long. Have you read the new Aboulela?...
body:
Layla!!

It's been too long. Have you finished the Leila Aboulela you mentioned? I just started it on your recommendation. Also — are you all coming to Houston this December? I want to plan a proper girls' dinner.

Miss you tons,
Bri

subject: Re: Miss you! + book recommendation
snippet: Almost done with it — beautiful prose. Yes, December in Houston!...
body:
Bri!

Almost done with it — the prose is just beautiful, very her. And yes, we're coming to Houston around the 20th of December. Girls' dinner is non-negotiable. I'll bring you some za'atar and a few books.

Love you,
Layla

subject: Running a few minutes late tomorrow
snippet: Good evening ma, the keke had a problem so I may be 15 mins late...
body:
Good evening ma,

The keke on my route had a problem this evening, so I may be about 15 minutes late tomorrow morning. I will still get Sophia ready for school in time. So sorry for any inconvenience.

Blessing

subject: Re: Running a few minutes late tomorrow
snippet: No problem at all Blessing, drive safe...
body: No problem at all, Blessing. Please don't rush — safety first. I'll get Sophia started on breakfast and you can take over when you arrive. Thank you for letting me know.\n\nLayla

subject: WAITA-EACRI yam proposal draft
snippet: Sharing the latest draft — comments in the margins, let's align before
body: Sharing the latest draft — comments in the margins, let's align before the deadline.
